item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, are a bunch of us in a "returning veteran authors posting new SCPs" arms race? If so, I approve, because the list is getting filled with top-notch articles like this. Easy +1 - spooky, fascinating, and ties in well with existing Fifthist characterization and lore.

item.title: Re: 3125 Headcanon
item.content:encoded:
are you trying to make SCP-3125 a Fifthist entity/Archon/Starfish

It's already heavily implied 3125 is related to Fifthism. The number itself is 55, the password is 55555, it was published (roughly) on May 5th (5/5), the fact 3125 caused 1425 (according to CASE COLOURLESS GREEN), the series it's in is called Five Five Five Five Five, the number 5 appears 5 times before the passcode, and 3125 is called a "five-dimensional entity" in Your Last First Day.

And, of course, this line from CASE COLOURLESS GREEN:

The other Wheeler presents her finished diagram. She has drawn a mutated, fractally complex grasping hand with fivefold symmetry. It has no wrist or arm, just five long human fingers pointing in five directions. At its core, there's a pentagonal opening which could be a mouth.

item.title: Re: 3125 Headcanon
item.content:encoded:
SEMI-UNRELATED EDIT: Is it only the staff team that can add tags?

No, but generally it's best to let them handle doing their job.

item.title: 3125 Headcanon
item.content:encoded:
Going off of the guess that all of the 3125 talk is about SCP-3125. Is this just you expanding your headcanon, or are you trying to make SCP-3125 a Fifthist entity/Archon/Starfish?

SEMI-UNRELATED EDIT: Is it only the staff team that can add tags?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if "Loss of fingernails" or "Finger expansion." is creepier. This is definitely the first time I've seen someone get lost in non-Euclidean biology, though, so upvote for that if nothing else!

Okay, but I also appreciate that this attempts to reconcile some of the star-based Fifthist imagery with the smoke thing, because that's always confused me, even after hearing the podcast about it. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love a good Fifthist SCP, and boy does this deliver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That autopsy log made me feel very uncomfortable in a good way, and the rest of this was the solid weirdness I've come to love Fifthism for.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: First try at something Fifthist! Thanks to TheGreatSea, CaptainKirby, PeppersGhost and NineVolt for their feedback in chat.
